old_claim.note: Oil Drilling provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Commercial petroleum refining depended on a crude-oil supply from productive oil wells, including the 1858 Ontario and 1859 Titusville wells tied to early refining chronology.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica states that crude-petroleum refining originated with successful oil wells in Ontario in 1858 and Titusville, Pennsylvania, in 1859.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source links the origin of crude-petroleum refining to successful oil wells.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Oil drilling is enabling because productive wells provide the crude-petroleum feedstock that made commercial refining a distinct industrial process.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: oil_drilling remains an enabling edge with textbook support.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to pre-oil-well coal-oil or shale-oil refining.
A stronger source showed commercial crude-petroleum refining preceded productive oil wells.
A dedicated crude_oil_supply node replaced oil_drilling as the graph prerequisite.
short_reason: Productive oil wells supply the crude petroleum feedstock.
